title: stenocalyx brasiliensis var. erythrocarpa
intro: Stenocalyx brasiliensis var. erythrocarpa, also known as the Brazilian red-fruit tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's bark, leaves, and fruits have been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Stenocalyx brasiliensis var. erythrocarpa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions and decoctions, are used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
